title: daphne oleoides subsp. hispanica
intro: Daphne oleoides subsp. hispanica, also known as Spanish laurel or prickly daphne, is a shrubby evergreen plant native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its fragrant, pinkish-white flowers and spiny, green leaves. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been employed to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of Daphne oleoides subsp. hispanica have been found to provide health benefits including pain relief, reduced inflammation, and antimicrobial effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are commonly available in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its therapeutic properties.
